---
output: github_document
bibliography: inst/REFERENCES.bib
link-citations: true
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r child = "man/rmd/common-setup.Rmd"}
```

```{r}
#| include: false
# code chunks
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.path = "man/figures/README-")
```

# midfielddata <img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="125K">

## Overview

midfielddata is an R data package that provides anonymized student-level records for 98,000 undergraduates at three US institutions from 1988 through 2018, collected in four data tables keyed by student ID:

- __student__ &nbsp; all students, their demographics, transfer status, institution, etc. 
- __course__  &nbsp; all students, every course, course name, number, credit hours, grades, etc. 
- __term__    &nbsp; all students, every term, program, standing, GPA, etc. 
- __degree__  &nbsp; all graduating students, their degree(s), program(s), term

The data in midfielddata are a proportionate stratified sample of the MIDFIELD database [@aee2016], but are not suitable for drawing inferences about program attributes or student experiences---midfielddata are for *practice*, not research. 

Suggested packages. 

- [midfieldr](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=midfieldr) for working with MIDFIELD data [@Layton+Long+Ohland:2026:midfieldr]
- [data.table](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=data.table) for manipulating data [@Dowle+Srinivasan:2026:data.table]


## Installation

The installed size of midfielddata is about 24 Mb, so installation will take longer than that of a conventional CRAN package. Install from the MIDFIELDR drat repository with: 

```{r}
#| eval: false
install.packages("midfielddata",
  repos = "https://MIDFIELDR.github.io/drat/",
  type = "source"
)
``` 

## Usage

```{r}
#| echo: false
library(midfielddata)
```

Data tables can be loaded individually or collectively as needed.

```{r}
#| eval: false
library(midfielddata)

# Load one table as needed
data(student)

# Or load multiple tables
data(course, term, degree)
```

Tutorials:

- We examine these data in greater detail in the package   [articles](https://midfieldr.github.io/midfielddata/articles/index.html). 
- How one *uses* these data is developed in detail on the  [midfieldr](https://midfieldr.github.io/midfieldr/) website.  

## Acknowledgments

The development of midfieldr and midfielddata was supported by the US National Science Foundation through grant numbers 1545667 and 2142087. 

```{r child = "man/rmd/common-closing.Rmd", eval = TRUE}
```
